topic: red wine structure and ageing capacity
trigger_keywords: estructura potente y necesidad de guarda

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A concentrated red wine begins with substantial tannin, acidity, fruit concentration, and, in fortified styles, alcohol and sugar.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Acidity and other stable components slow deterioration while tannins polymerise and the concentrated flavour profile develops over time.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine has the structural capacity for extended ageing, during which tannins can integrate and tertiary complexity can emerge; structure enables ageing but does not guarantee improvement under poor storage.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The wine has the structural capacity for extended ageing, during which tannins can integrate and tertiary complexity can emerge; structure enables ageing but does not guarantee improvement under poor storage.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
